subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Critically evaluate the multi-dimensional nature of poverty using Social Exclusion and Vulnerability frameworks
Analyze the methodological limitations of the Poverty Line in a globalized economy
Deconstruct the intersectionality of caste, gender, and economic status in chronic poverty
Assess the impact of colonial history and economic policy on current inter-state disparities
Evaluate the 'Growth-Targeted' strategy of the Indian government and its implementation hurdles
Synthesize the transition from Income Poverty to the broader concept of Human Dignity

lesson_plan.title: Sociology of Deprivation: Exclusion & Vulnerability
topics_covered: Theoretical frameworks: Social Exclusion as both cause and consequence
Measuring Vulnerability: Asset ownership, education, and health risks
Human Poverty Index vs. Income Poverty
teaching_strategy: Socratic Seminar on 'The invisibility of the urban poor'
Comparative analysis of structural barriers in Rural vs. Urban settings
student_activities: Draft an analytical thesis on how social exclusion perpetuates the poverty cycle
Analyze the 'Vulnerability' of landless laborers to climate-induced shocks
assessment: Critical essay on 'Social Exclusion in Modern India'

lesson_plan.title: Deconstructing the Poverty Line Methodology
topics_covered: Critical review of the Calorie-based approach (2400/2100)
Monetary thresholds and the Purchasing Power Parity (PPP) concept
Global standards: The World Bank’s $1.90/day methodology
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the 'Paradox of Calorie vs. Income' in urban settings
Analysis of the role of the NSSO in national data collection
student_activities: Calculate and compare the poverty line across different Indian states using raw data
Debate: 'Is the current poverty line a measure of survival or dignity?'
assessment: Numerical problem set on poverty line thresholds

lesson_plan.title: Intersectionality of Vulnerable Groups
topics_covered: Social identities (SC/ST) and economic marginalization
Gendered poverty: Why women are the 'poorest of the poor'
Dependency ratios and household resource allocation
teaching_strategy: Advanced data interpretation of poverty ratio graphs
student_activities: Analyze the intersection of caste and landlessness in poverty data
Construct a 'Vulnerability Matrix' for different demographic groups
assessment: Critique of the household inequality model

lesson_plan.title: Geopolitics & Inter-State Disparities
topics_covered: The 'Laggard' states: Structural reasons for stagnation in Bihar/Odisha
Success stories: Land reforms in WB vs. HRD in Kerala
Global Trends: East Asian 'Tiger' economies vs. Sub-Saharan Africa
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the impact of the Green Revolution on regional inequality
student_activities: Plot a multi-variable graph showing poverty decline vs. education spend across states
Write a comparative report on poverty reduction in China and India

lesson_plan.title: Historical & Economic Roots of Poverty
topics_covered: The Colonial 'Drain Theory' and de-industrialization
Economic stagnation vs. Population growth post-1947
Unequal asset distribution and the 'Debt Trap'
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the long-term effects of British agricultural policies
Discussion on the 'Vicious Cycle' of rural indebtedness
student_activities: Flowchart the historical transition from colonial rule to modern poverty
Analyze a case study on small-scale farming and the cost of capital
assessment: Short answer test on historical causes

lesson_plan.title: Policy Audit: Anti-Poverty Measures
topics_covered: Critique of Targeted Anti-Poverty Programs (TAPP)
MGNREGA 2005: Successes, failures, and implementation leakages
The role of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) and Aadhar in targeting
teaching_strategy: Group brainstorming on 'Why benefits do not reach the poor'
Detailed audit of the Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY)
student_activities: Draft a policy brief to improve the implementation of MGNREGA in your district
Evaluate the impact of the Mid-day Meal scheme on human capital

lesson_plan.title: The Road Ahead: Human Dignity & Synthesis
topics_covered: Broadening the definition: From survival to human rights
The role of health, education, and gender equality in permanent exit from poverty
Chapter revision and synthesis
student_activities: Participate in a whole-chapter 'Grand Revision' seminar
Complete a summary synthesis chart in notebooks
assessment: Comprehensive unit test (30 marks) featuring case-study based questions

teaching_aids: Complex data sets on Gini coefficient and state poverty ratios
Bar graphs of vulnerable social groups
Case study handouts on Land Reform and MGNREGA audits
evaluation_methods: Advanced Data Interpretation and Numerical Solving
teacher_tip: Challenge students to look beyond the 'handouts'—emphasize that sustainable poverty reduction requires a structural shift in 'voice' and 'agency' for the marginalized, not just caloric provision.
